# Concrete Knowhow

> Free, accurate construction material calculators for concrete, gravel, mulch, topsoil, sand, and sod. All calculations run in the browser — no sign-up, no data collection.

Concrete Knowhow helps homeowners and contractors estimate material quantities for outdoor and construction projects. Enter dimensions, get instant results in cubic yards, tons, or bag counts.

## Calculators

- [Concrete Slab Calculator](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/concrete/): Cubic yards and bag counts (60 lb / 80 lb) for rectangular or round slabs. Includes ready-mix advisory above 1 yd³.
- [Gravel Calculator](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/gravel/): Cubic yards and tons for driveways, paths, and drainage. Five material types with bulk density conversion.
- [Mulch Calculator](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/mulch/): Cubic yards plus 2 cu ft and 3 cu ft bag counts for garden beds and landscape areas.
- [Topsoil Calculator](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/topsoil/): Cubic yards with 10–15% settling allowance so you order enough to end up at your target depth.
- [Sand Calculator](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/sand/): Cubic yards and tons for leveling, fill, and paver bases. Five sand types with per-type bulk density.
- [Sod Calculator](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/sod/): Square footage, rolls (10 sq ft each), and pallets (450 sq ft each) for any lawn area.

## Key Formulas

- Rectangular volume: L(ft) × W(ft) × D(ft) = cubic feet; ÷ 27 = cubic yards
- Circular volume: π × (diameter/2)² × depth(ft) = cubic feet
- Concrete: 60 lb bag = 0.45 cu ft; 80 lb bag = 0.60 cu ft
- Mulch: standard bag at Lowe's / Home Depot = 2 cu ft
- Gravel/sand weight: cubic yards × bulk density (t/yd³)
- Sod: 1 roll = 10 sq ft; 1 pallet = 450 sq ft

## Optional

- [Full reference with formulas, thresholds, and FAQ answers](https://concrete.mrknowitall.net/llms-full.txt)
